reviews.review_msg: Professor Ghavami is a great lecturer and includes tons of added details in lectures from his experience in the industry. I highly recommend him, though I think he could improve his course organisation.
BEST prof ever! He is really nice, crystal clear notes, excellent explanations, interesting, knowledgeable, helpful, approachable and open to answering any question. Take her class if you can.
Fantastic professor. It's difficult to forget what he says because of how he says it. Cares about students. He's the best.

Ameer is an absolutely amazing professor. He gave us many hints regarding obtaining a career in the industry. He restructured the course and added many quizzes which helped me and my classmates gradually understand the topics and therefore pass the course. He is passionate about hardware design; making the course more interesting.

Honestly, Ameer just reads off the slides in a disinterested tone, and as long as you do the labs you'll be fine. He also likes to leave class early during quizzes as he is too "busy" to ensure academic integrity.
Just took ENSC350 with him. A quiz every lecture makes you do the work.
One of the top professors SFU had. But he left. I can't accept that from SFU. He really cared about his students and ensured not only they learn, but they also participate.
well he is a tough grader now earlier he was easy
He is the sweetest professor at SFU. He is very helpful and shows interest in teaching. Exams are very fair and you can get A+ by attending every class.
so much information in lectures. Will pretty much let you know what to expect in exams. Assignments are time consuming but rewarding. Awesome prof. very helpful and funny.
Expect a 10 page final exam. Labs are heavy and time consuming that happens every few weeks. Memorize lecture slides for her exams - really helpful.

I love Lucky, such a breath of fresh air to have a prof who actually cares about students and is also passionate about his work. ENSC 350 is a difficult class, but honestly you can do well if you literally just take the time to study. I highly recommend going to class as he always drops hints and for assignments, exams, and the projects.

Lucky is a strict but fair teacher and rewards students who participate in class. Although lectures are not mandatory, he'll often mention tips for exams and assignments, so it's best to attend and pay attention. 350 is a difficult, and Lucky will ensure you learn the material one way or another. My only gripe is a lack of resources for the DPs/FP

Lectures are the most important aspect of his course, make sure to listen, take good notes and try to attend all of them. He does not make answers available to any of his study material, but if you can demonstrate knowledge on the exams (write as much as you can), you can still get a good grade. Workload of this course is pretty high. Overall 2.5/5

The key to success is to attend the lectures. Some of his notes will not make much sense unless you hear him explain them and his thought process on why he designed the notes like that. Exams are weighted the most and should be your priority. The highlight of the class are his lectures. Very engaging, funny, and you will learn a lot. Don't skip!

Lucky has an exam-focused style of teaching; lectures & asking questions are a must for learning the material. Thankfully, he is extremely knowledgeable and tries to give students a deeper perspective on their questions. People's main gripe is that the exams are difficult, but he marks VERY generously based on what you understand, so pay attention!

Lucky has a more classical teaching approach that I've found to be genuinely helpful in understanding and applying the material in this course. If you fall behind and do not make the effort to either catch up on your own or reach out to peers/TAs/Lucky, you will find that the course is incredibly difficult. Stick to studying, and ask questions.
